name: Coca and Eresma Valley Greenway Loop
name_original: Castillo de Coca y torre mudéjar de San Nicolás — circular por la vía verde del valle del Eresma desde Coca
description: Cycle an easy 9.4-mile loop on the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma, passing Coca Castle and the Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Coca, Spain, offers routes through the flat, agricultural landscapes of the Castilian plateau. The region is characterized by river valleys, including the Eresma and Voltoya, and interspersed pine forests. Terrain generally features minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for relaxed cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Coca The most popular touring cycling route is Coca and Eresma Valley Greenway Loop, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This route follows the Eresma Valley, offering views of the river and surrounding agricultural fields. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás – Coca Castle loop from Coca, a difficult 30.6 miles (49.3 km) path. This route features the historic Coca Castle and includes varied terrain with some elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Nava de la Asunción – Río Voltoya Crossing loop from Nava de la Asunción, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail leading through the Voltoya River valley, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Coca is defined by flat agricultural plains, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging loops. The routes in Coca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 65 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Coca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Coca?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in the Coca area, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, relaxed rides to more challenging loops.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Coca?
A: The terrain around Coca is generally flat, characterized by agricultural landscapes, river valleys, and interspersed pine forests. You'll primarily encounter minimal elevation changes, making it ideal for relaxed touring cycling. Routes often follow the Eresma and Voltoya rivers.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Coca?
A: Yes, Coca offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107542369/coca-and-eresma-valley-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca and Eresma Valley Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 9.4-mile trail that follows the Eresma Valley, providing gentle gradients and scenic views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling in Coca?
A: Many routes in Coca incorporate historical and natural attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/908407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3711912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5783190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the Coca area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás – Coca Castle loop from Coca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.6-mile path with varied terrain and some elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;☕️ Bar Bulevar – Pine Forests of Segovia loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a longer distance and more significant elevation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Coca?
A: The touring cycling routes in Coca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil agricultural landscapes, the scenic river valleys, and the well-maintained paths that offer a relaxing and enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Coca?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Coca are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107542369/coca-and-eresma-valley-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca and Eresma Valley Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31490211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava de la Asunción – Río Voltoya Crossing loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Coca?
A: The region around Coca, Spain, experiences a continental climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with milder weather and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters can be cold, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there routes that follow river paths in Coca?
A: Absolutely. The Eresma and Voltoya rivers are prominent features of the landscape, and many touring cycling routes follow their valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31490211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava de la Asunción – Río Voltoya Crossing loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through the Voltoya River valley.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through pine forests in Coca?
A: Yes, the region features interspersed pine forests. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;☕️ Bar Bulevar – Pine Forests of Segovia loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt; will guide you through these wooded areas, offering a different kind of scenery compared to the open agricultural plains.

Q: Are there options for moderate difficulty touring cycling routes in Coca?
A: Yes, the majority of routes in Coca are classified as moderate, with 15 such options available. These routes offer a good balance for cyclists looking for a bit more distance or varied terrain without extreme challenges. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio El Batán – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Well loop from Santiuste de San Juan Bautista&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The Mudejar Tower of San Nicolás is the only remainder of the old homonymous church that was left in ruins in the 18th century. With some difficulty, you can access the highest part, where there is an excellent viewpoint of the town.
It was built in the 15th century and is considered one of the best examples of Spanish Gothic-Mudejar. Property of the Casa De Alba, transferred to the Ministry of Agriculture of Spain since 1954.
Picturesque section of the greenway of the eresma that advances between cultivated fields. The most beautiful time is in spring when the red poppies bloom, standing out among the greenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route in Coca?
faqs.answer: You can conveniently park near the old railway station in Coca, which is also close to the Forest Museum. This area serves as an excellent starting point for your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families with children or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is classified as an easy bike ride with a low difficulty level. It's predominantly on compacted soil and paved tracks, making it very smooth and safe for touring bicycles, families, and even individuals with reduced mobility. The Vía Verde design ensures gentle gradients and no motorized traffic.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with spring bringing vibrant poppy fields. Summer can be warm, so consider early morning or late afternoon rides. Winter rides are also possible, but check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on Vías Verdes, but they should always be kept on a leash to ensure the safety and comfort of other users and to protect local wildlife. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. Vías Verdes are public access routes designed for recreational use.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features compacted soil and mostly paved tracks, offering a very smooth and comfortable ride for touring bicycles. As it incorporates a section of a former railway line (Vía Verde), the gradients are gentle, making it very accessible.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights. The most prominent is the magnificent Coca Castle, a stunning Mudéjar-Gothic fortress. You'll also see the Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás and cycle through picturesque Poppy Fields on the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma, especially in spring. The route itself, being a Vía Verde, offers views of the Eresma and Voltoya rivers, pine forests, and agricultural fields.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 15.15 kilometers long. For an average touring cyclist, it typically takes around 1 hour and 1 minute to complete, allowing for a relaxed pace to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route follows the Vía Verde del Eresma and the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma. These greenways are part of a larger network, with the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma connecting Segovia with Olmedo, spanning approximately 73 kilometers in total. You'll also briefly intersect with the Madrid Way of Saint James and the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde itself is generally a rural path, the starting point in Coca offers amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities in the town of Coca. It's always a good idea to bring your own water and snacks, especially for the sections away from the town center.

faqs.question: What makes this Vía Verde route particularly accessible for all users?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma is designed with universal accessibility in mind. Its gentle gradients, smooth surfaces, and absence of motorized traffic make it ideal for cyclists of all ages and abilities, including those with reduced mobility. It's a safe and comfortable environment to enjoy the outdoors.

waypoints.name: Poppy Fields on the Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma
